feature_name: v1.18.0 "iOS playability + player polish" -- a 3-FR focused branch: iOS mp4 codec-based playability (yt-dlp downloads + manually-added files), persistent-player stale-poster/FOUC polish, and a restored rescan pending-transcode list
current_tasks_note: Design complete; EM finalized the task breakdown into T1-T4 and routed to Implementation. SHARED working tree: agents must not touch the same file concurrently. File map -- T1=lib/ytdlp/args.js(+test); T2=server.js+pure helpers(+tests); T3=public/js/player.js(+test); T4=server.js+public/js/setup.js. T1/T2/T3 file sets are pairwise DISJOINT => START IN PARALLEL. T4 both DEPENDS ON T2 (must consume T2's generalized codec-aware needsTranscode/transcodeStatus, not a divergent path) AND edits server.js (shared with T2) => BLOCKED, start only after T2 is merged/verified. current_tasks=[T1,T2,T3]; T4 pending. Inbox files: .state/inbox/software-developer-T1.md ... -T4.md (each self-contained). Coordinator (EM) owns ALL git -- SDEs report files-changed + Node 22 lint/test only, do NOT commit.
branch: feature/v1.18-ios-playability (off main, which is at v1.17.1)
base: main (v1.17.1). Prior shipped records live in docs/exec-plans/completed/ (most recently v1.17-polish).
git_ownership_note: Coordinator (EM) owns ALL git. SDEs must NOT commit/stage/push. SDE reports files-changed + test output; coordinator commits per task.
node_toolchain_note: Node 22 is the toolchain standard (engines >=20). build-verify MUST run tests on Node 22; fnm/nvm PATH export needed before any npm/node command in this repo per CLAUDE.md.
process_plan: FULL PIPELINE, RIGHT-SIZED. FR-1 (both sub-items) touches two security-sensitive spawn surfaces (the yt-dlp arg-array builder in lib/ytdlp/args.js, and the ffprobe-on-user-files extension + the transcode spawn's now-larger trigger surface in server.js) -- these get FOCUSED REVIEW ATTENTION even though no existing guard is loosened (no new guard removed, format-selection args only added to an already-arg-array builder; ffprobe extension only adds fields parsed from an already-running probe). PM recommends EM/Design confirm the exact review tier per item at task-breakdown time -- likely a lighter single-QA-plus-focused-attention tier for FR-1a/FR-1b (not a full two-reviewer/adversarial gate, since no guard is removed or weakened), matching the touchpoint-attention pattern used for v1.17.0's FR-3/FR-6/FR-8. FR-2 and FR-3 are additive polish/bugfix items with no security-guard surface -- LIGHTER single-QA no-regression review. Dean's on-device iPhone pass is the documented, non-negotiable ARBITER for FR-1 (actual iOS playability of both yt-dlp downloads and codec-flagged manual files) and FR-2 (perceived smoothness / no stale-poster flash, especially on Next) -- no headless-browser/E2E test infra exists in this repo (per docs/RELIABILITY.md), so on-device verification is the only real arbiter for those two, not a testing gap. FR-3 is fully code/test-verifiable (API response shape + UI wiring) with a light visual/legibility on-device check. Ship cadence: merge -> tag v1.18.0 -> Docker publish -> Dean's on-device pass (esp. FR-1 real HEVC/AC-3/VP9-webm fixtures and FR-2's Next-button transition).
hard_constraints: NO REGRESSIONS. Every existing extension-based transcode case (.avi/.flv/.wmv/.mpg/.mpeg) must keep working identically through FR-1b's generalized needsTranscode -- test/unit/pure-helpers.test.js's existing cases are the regression bar. The existing desktop live-transcode (?live=1) path, the mobile lazy-transcode + preparing-overlay + poll, the transcode cache's size-capped LRU eviction, and the single-worker transcode queue are all reused UNCHANGED -- only the set of files that can enter that pipeline grows.

FR-1a: every existing yt-dlp security constraint stays byte-identical -- arg-array/no-shell construction, the '--' separator immediately before positional targets, the ALLOWED_HOSTS channel-URL allowlist (lib/ytdlp/url.js), SF4 path confinement (resolveChannelDir/isPathUnder), --download-archive dedup vs. the one-off --no-download-archive/--force-overwrites bypass, --windows-filenames, the Shorts --match-filter gate, and normalizeQuality/assertFormat/normalizeFiletype's allowlist-or-safe-default posture. This task ONLY adds format-selection/preference args to the existing buildYtdlpDownloadArgs builder.

FR-1b: codec probing at scan time piggybacks on the ALREADY-RUNNING per-file ffprobe call (server.js extractMetadataAndThumbnail, line ~899) -- no new spawn. Actual transcoding stays LAZY/on-demand (mirrors the existing AVI-on-mobile flow) -- this round must NOT eagerly transcode a whole library on scan. A per-item codec-probe failure must degrade safely (never crash the scan, never falsely flag needsTranscode on bad/missing data) per docs/RELIABILITY.md's 'never let one bad file take down a scan' standard.

FR-1b's browser-compatible codec allowlist is H.264/AVC video + AAC audio ONLY -- deliberately conservative (HEVC and VP9/AV1 are NOT allowlisted despite partial device support), per Dean's own stated intent.

FR-2: no new page reload is introduced; player.js load()'s existing 'adopt' (same-media, e.g. dock-to-full expand) vs. genuine-new-load branching is preserved unchanged -- the poster/frame visual reset applies only within the existing teardownMediaState()/setupForMedia() sequence for genuine new loads. Every other persistent-player behavior (playback continuity across in-app navigation, dock<->full transitions, iOS inline playback, Media Session, resume overlay, transcode preparing-overlay + polling) must keep working identically.

FR-3: the restored pending-transcode list must be derived from the SAME generalized needsTranscode/transcodeStatus logic FR-1b produces -- not a second, divergent detection path. The response payload must be bounded (not an unbounded names array) given FR-1b will likely flag more files than the old extension-only set on a large library.

follow docs/CONTRIBUTING.md: vanilla DOM (no framework/bundler), CommonJS server, 2-space/semicolons/single-quotes; textContent over innerHTML for NEW dynamic strings (FR-3's rendered file names/titles especially -- user-controlled data must never hit innerHTML); node:test for new/regression tests (FR-1b's codec detection unit-tested with MOCKED ffprobe output, since ffmpeg isn't installed in CI); lint 0 warnings; NO new runtime dependencies.

Preserve the era-theme system (CSS custom properties / --vars). Any new CSS (FR-2's neutral-placeholder treatment, FR-3's restored transcode-list styling in Setup) must use existing theme tokens; no hardcoded colors that break the 2005/2009/2014/2021 eras or light/dark modes.

No headless-browser/E2E test infrastructure exists in this repo (per docs/RELIABILITY.md) -- this bounds what FR-1 (real-device iOS playability) and FR-2 (perceived smoothness/no-flash) can claim as [UNIT]/[INTEGRATION]; Dean's on-device iPhone pass is the documented arbiter for both, not a rigor gap. Every pure helper each fix produces (codec-allowlist check, ffprobe-stream-parsing, any player visual-state-reset helper, the pending-transcode-list derivation) still gets real unit coverage.

product_forks_for_dean.fr1a_recode_fallback: RESOLVED per Dean's own stated intent, not an open fork. Format-selection (prefer an already-H.264/AAC stream via -S/-f) is primary; --recode-video mp4 is only an optional fallback when no compatible stream exists at all (recoding re-encodes -- real CPU/time cost on a home server). PE confirms exact -S/-f syntax and whether the fallback is worth the added complexity.
product_forks_for_dean.fr1a_format_preference_scope: OPEN, non-blocking, a NEW fork surfaced during Discovery (not pre-resolved by Dean's brief). buildYtdlpDownloadArgs only forces --merge-output-format mp4 when sub.filetype resolves to the explicit 'mp4' value; 'default' (today: no container flag, yt-dlp's natural choice) is a separate, arguably more common source of VP9/AV1-in-webm downloads. RECOMMEND applying the H.264/AAC format-sort preference (-S) to EVERY video download regardless of filetype selection (a soft stream preference, not a container-forcing flag -- low risk even for 'default'/'mkv'/'webm' selections), while leaving --merge-output-format's explicit-'mp4'-only trigger unchanged. Needs PE/Dean confirmation at Design.
product_forks_for_dean.fr1b_lazy_vs_eager: RESOLVED per Dean's own stated intent and docs/ARCHITECTURE.md's existing 'lazy transcoding' key decision, not an open fork. Codec probing at scan time (free, piggybacked on the existing ffprobe call); actual transcoding stays lazy/on-demand, reusing the existing single-worker queue + cache LRU eviction.
product_forks_for_dean.fr1b_codec_allowlist: RESOLVED per Dean's own stated intent, not an open fork. H.264/AVC + AAC only -- deliberately conservative; HEVC and VP9/AV1 excluded despite partial device support.
product_forks_for_dean.fr2_placeholder_mechanism: OPEN, non-blocking, a NEW fork surfaced during Discovery. No placeholder image asset exists anywhere in public/assets/ today (confirmed by inspection -- same 'not a trivial one-line asset fix' flag as v1.17.0's FR-7 download-icon precedent). RECOMMEND a CSS-only treatment (no new asset, era-theme-token-friendly) as the lowest-risk option; a blank-image poster or a new branded placeholder graphic are viable Design-stage alternatives if a CSS-only treatment proves insufficient on-device.
product_forks_for_dean.fr3_payload_size: OPEN, non-blocking, a NEW fork surfaced during Discovery. FR-1b's codec-based detection can flag substantially more files than the old extension-only set on a large library -- an uncapped pending-transcode names list in GET /api/scan-status's JSON response could grow large. RECOMMEND a cap (e.g. first N names + a '+K more' remainder count) rather than an unbounded list. Exact N left to Design.

FR-1.title: iOS mp4 playability (ANCHOR) -- codec-based transcode detection replacing extension-only detection
FR-1.review_weight: PM-recommended focused-review-attention tier (security-sensitive touchpoints: yt-dlp arg-array spawn core for FR-1a; ffprobe-on-user-files extension + the transcode spawn's larger trigger surface for FR-1b) -- not necessarily a full two-reviewer/adversarial gate since no existing guard is loosened, but EM/Design should confirm at task-breakdown time. Dean on-device iPhone pass is the ARBITER for actual playability.
FR-1.current_behavior_confirmed: needsTranscode(ext) (server.js line 229-231) only fires for TRANSCODE_EXTENSIONS = ['.avi','.flv','.wmv','.mpg','.mpeg'] -- every .mp4/.mkv/.mov/.webm/.m4v is assumed browser-playable regardless of its actual codec (reconcileTranscode, line 825-844, and the scan-time seed at line 1193, both key off this same ext-only function). buildYtdlpDownloadArgs (lib/ytdlp/args.js) selects video streams via a plain QUALITY_SELECTORS map (bestvideo+bestaudio/best, height-capped) with no codec preference, and only sets --merge-output-format when sub.filetype resolves to an explicit non-'default' container. extractMetadataAndThumbnail (server.js line 888-934) already runs ffprobe at scan time for duration+tags (line 899) but does not read stream codec info.

sub_items.FR-1a: yt-dlp downloads: add format-selection preference (-S vcodec:h264,acodec:aac and/or an -f selector) to buildYtdlpDownloadArgs, preserving --merge-output-format mp4's existing explicit-'mp4'-trigger and all existing security constraints (arg-array, '--' separator, host allowlist, SF4 confinement, archive dedup, --windows-filenames, skip-shorts, one-off-vs-subscription). Optional --recode-video mp4 fallback only when no compatible stream exists.
sub_items.FR-1b: manually-added files: extend the scan's existing ffprobe call to also read stream codec_name/codec_type; generalize needsTranscode from extension-only to extension-OR-codec (H.264/AAC allowlist; HEVC/AC-3/DTS/E-AC-3/etc. flagged); reuse the existing lazy on-demand transcode pipeline (single-worker queue, cache LRU eviction) -- codec probing at scan is free (piggybacks on the existing ffprobe call), but actual transcoding stays lazy, never eager-on-scan.

files: server.js (needsTranscode/TRANSCODE_EXTENSIONS ~line 227-231, reconcileTranscode ~line 825-844, extractMetadataAndThumbnail's ffprobe call ~line 899, the scan-time seed ~line 1193)
FR-1.arbiter: Dean on-device (iPhone/iOS Safari) for actual playability of both a yt-dlp-downloaded video and a manually-added HEVC/AC-3 file; code/test-verifiable for the arg-builder and codec-allowlist logic itself

FR-2.title: Player polish: stale poster + FOUC on Next (persistent-player visual reset)
FR-2.review_weight: LIGHTER single-QA no-regression -- Dean on-device (iOS Safari) is the ARBITER for the smoothness/no-flash feel
FR-2.current_behavior_confirmed: player.js's teardownMediaState() (line 970-987) clears audio-bg-art and skip controls on every load but never resets the <video> element's visible poster/last-rendered frame. setupForMedia() (line 989-1040) only sets mediaPlayer.poster for the AUDIO branch (line 995, '/thumbnail/'+id) -- the VIDEO branch never sets or clears a poster at all, so the previous video's last decoded frame lingers until the new source decodes its own first frame. Dean also reports a FOUC specifically on the prev/next Next-button-driven advance.
FR-2.fix: On every genuine new load() (not the adopt same-media dock<->full path), immediately reset the visible poster/art to a neutral static placeholder BEFORE the new media source is assigned -- clearing both the video frame/poster and the audio-bg-art -- so there is no flash of the outgoing item's image during the transition. No new reload; load()'s existing adopt/non-adopt branching unchanged; every other persistent-player behavior (playback continuity, dock/full transitions, iOS inline, Media Session, resume overlay, transcode overlay) preserved.
FR-2.arbiter: Dean on-device (iOS Safari), specifically the prev/next Next-button transition -- no headless-browser infra exists to automate this

FR-3.title: Rescan should list what it'll transcode (regression restore, pairs with FR-1b)
FR-3.review_weight: LIGHTER single-QA no-regression; payload-bounding gets focused review attention
FR-3.current_behavior_confirmed: GET /api/scan-status (server.js line 1675-1688) returns only a COUNT (transcoding, filtered on needsTranscode + an in-flight transcodeStatus) -- no names/list. public/js/setup.js's pollScanStatus (line 294-335) renders only 'Converting N file(s) in the background...' -- no per-file detail. ROADMAP.md's 'Bugs & regressions' section confirms this used to surface the actual file list and regressed.
FR-3.fix: Restore the actual pending-transcode SET (count + names/titles), derived from the SAME generalized needsTranscode/transcodeStatus logic FR-1b produces (not a second, divergent detection path), surfaced in Setup's #scan-status line via the same polling path. Bound the payload (cap + remainder count) since FR-1b will likely flag more files than the old extension-only set on a large library.
files: public/css/style.css (list styling, era tokens, if needed)
test/unit / test/integration (scan-status payload shape, empty-set guard)
FR-3.arbiter: code/test-verifiable (API payload shape, empty-set guard, textContent-not-innerHTML rendering); light Dean visual/legibility check, mobile included given the branch's iOS focus

pe_design_items: Route to Design after Discovery. Items needing PE design: FR-1 (both sub-items -- FR-1a's exact yt-dlp format-selection/-f syntax and the resolved format-preference scope fork; FR-1b's ffprobe-stream-parsing approach, the generalized needsTranscode signature, and confirming the ffprobe-shell-string touchpoint isn't widened), FR-2 (the exact neutral-placeholder mechanism -- CSS-only vs. blank-poster vs. new asset -- and where in load()'s sequence the reset must land to beat the new source's first paint). FR-3 does not need PE design (a direct API-payload + UI-rendering extension of existing code) but still rides through Design for one cohesive plan and because it depends on FR-1b's generalized detection logic landing first.
cross_cutting: FR-3 depends on FR-1b's generalized needsTranscode/transcodeStatus logic (must reuse it, not fork a second detection path) -- sequencing note for EM's task breakdown. FR-1a and FR-1b are otherwise independent of each other and of FR-2/FR-3 (no shared files). FR-2 is fully isolated to public/js/player.js + public/css/style.css. No new deps; era-theme CSS vars for all new styling; textContent-not-innerHTML for new dynamic strings (FR-3's rendered filenames especially); node:test (FR-1b's codec detection unit-tested with MOCKED ffprobe output -- ffmpeg itself stays out of CI per docs/RELIABILITY.md); lint 0. No headless-browser/E2E infra exists (docs/RELIABILITY.md) -- FR-1 and FR-2 lean on Dean's on-device iPhone pass as the documented arbiter, not a testing gap; FR-3 ships fully code/test-verifiable ACs per docs/CONTRIBUTING.md's 'every new feature or bugfix ships with tests' standard.

tasks.title: yt-dlp iOS-compatible H.264/AAC format sort
tasks.description: In lib/ytdlp/args.js buildYtdlpDownloadArgs, add a module-level exported fixed literal `const VIDEO_FORMAT_SORT = 'vcodec:h264,acodec:aac';` (mirroring the SHORTS_MATCH_FILTER pattern) and, in the VIDEO branch only, push `'-S', VIDEO_FORMAT_SORT` immediately after the existing `args.push('-f', QUALITY_SELECTORS[quality]);` and before --merge-output-format / the -- separator. Soft sort => applies to EVERY video download and EVERY quality tier incl 'default'/'mkv'/'webm' (fork #2 recommended scope); does NOT exclude non-avc1 formats (2160p/1440p VP9/AV1-only tiers still resolve to best-available). --merge-output-format's explicit-'mp4'-only trigger stays byte-identical. Audio branch (-x --audio-format) untouched. --recode-video fallback DEFERRED (PE-recommended defer -- non-blocking Dean confirm). Fixed literal, never interpolated -- no injection surface.
tasks.done_when: -S VIDEO_FORMAT_SORT emitted for every QUALITY_SELECTORS tier in the video branch; audio branch unaffected; --merge-output-format trigger + --/host-allowlist/archive-dedup/--windows-filenames/shorts-match-filter/one-off-vs-subscription all byte-identical and covered by extended ytdlp-args tests; lint 0; Node 22 tests green.
tasks.gate: two-reviewer (QA + adversarial /code-review) -- spawn arg-array core; prove no guard moved

tasks.title: Codec probe + codec-aware needsTranscode + lazy transcode wiring
tasks.description: server.js: (1) extend extractMetadataAndThumbnail's existing scan-time ffprobe -show_entries from `format=duration:format_tags` to `format=duration:format_tags:stream=codec_name,codec_type` (same single JSON probe, no new spawn) AND harden THAT ONE call from shell-string exec() to execFile('ffprobe',[args...]) with filePath as a distinct arg-array element (removes path interpolation on the edited line; leave the two other ffmpeg art/frame exec() calls as tracked tech-debt). (2) New pure helpers next to parseFfprobeTags: parseFfprobeStreams(input) (accepts parsed obj OR raw stdout, JSON.parse in try/catch, returns {} on malformed, never throws; returns {videoCodec,audioCodec} = lowercased codec_name of first video/audio stream, undefined if absent); exported allowlist constants PLAYABLE_VIDEO_CODECS=new Set(['h264','avc1']), PLAYABLE_AUDIO_CODECS=new Set(['aac']); codecNeedsTranscode(videoCodec,audioCodec) => true only on POSITIVE id of a non-allowlisted codec (undefined/missing => false, so a failed probe never falsely flags). (3) Generalize needsTranscode to needsTranscode(ext, videoCodec, audioCodec) with codec args OPTIONAL: extension check first (byte-identical one-arg behavior), then codecNeedsTranscode. Update reconcileTranscode call site to pass item.videoCodec/item.audioCodec (authoritative recompute after probe attaches codecs); scan-time seed ~line 1193 stays ext-only. (4) Item model gains videoCodec/audioCodec (lowercased string, or null when probed-but-absent, property absent for legacy/un-probed); extend the reuse-unchanged-metadata guard (~server.js:1156) so a VIDEO item is only reused when it already carries codec fields => legacy entries re-probed ONCE to backfill (probe-only, never eager transcode). Audio items short-circuit. (5) NO new lazy-transcode code -- codec-flagged files ride the EXISTING reconcileTranscode->/video/:id->queueTranscode path; player already branches on needsTranscode/transcodeStatus. NEVER eager-transcode on scan; per-item probe failure degrades safely (no crash, no false flag).
tasks.done_when: parseFfprobeStreams + allowlist constants + codecNeedsTranscode + generalized needsTranscode implemented and unit-tested with MOCKED ffprobe output (parser robustness, allowlist H.264+AVC/AAC, degrade-safe on bad/missing data, ALL existing pure-helpers extension cases still pass unchanged); integration test for reconcileTranscode status transitions on an HEVC-.mp4 fixture; execFile hardening on the one probe line; item backfill guard in place; no eager scan-time transcode path added; lint 0; Node 22 tests green.
tasks.gate: two-reviewer (QA + adversarial /code-review) -- ffprobe-on-user-files extension + larger transcode-spawn trigger surface; prove no guard moved / no widened injection surface

tasks.title: Player stale-poster / FOUC visual reset on Next
tasks.description: public/js/player.js teardownMediaState(): after the existing mediaPlayer.pause(), add a visual reset that runs on every GENUINE new load (the adopt / dock<->full path returns early in load() before teardownMediaState() is called, so continuity is preserved untouched): mediaPlayer.removeAttribute('poster') (clears the audio branch's /thumbnail/<prevId> poster) then mediaPlayer.removeAttribute('src'); mediaPlayer.load() to drop the last-decoded video frame => element repaints nothing, revealing the existing #000 .player-container background (CSS-only neutral placeholder, NO new image asset, NO new CSS). Keep the existing audio-bg-art clear (~line 983). setupForMedia assigns the fresh src as today. Use removeAttribute('src') NOT src='' (avoids the empty-string-resolves-to-page-URL reload quirk). This is a media-element load(), NOT a page reload; does NOT touch load()'s adopt/non-adopt branching. Preserve ALL persistent-player behavior: playback continuity across navigation, dock/full transitions, iOS inline (playsinline), Media Session, resume overlay, transcode preparing overlay+poll.
tasks.done_when: poster/frame + audio-bg-art reset to neutral BEFORE new source assignment on every genuine non-adopt load; no new reload; adopt branching unchanged; no new CSS/asset (uses #000 container); if any pure visual-reset helper is cleanly extractable it gets node:test coverage (PE notes none is cleanly extractable from the IIFE => leans on Dean on-device arbiter); lint 0; Node 22 tests green.
tasks.gate: LIGHTER single-QA no-regression; Dean on-device (iOS Safari, Next-button transition) is the ARBITER

tasks.title: Rescan pending-transcode list (bounded)
tasks.description: BLOCKED ON T2 -- must consume T2's generalized codec-aware needsTranscode/transcodeStatus (NOT a second divergent detection path) AND edits server.js which T2 also edits. server.js GET /api/scan-status: derive the pending set from the SAME filter that already produces `transcoding` -- items.filter(i => i.needsTranscode && i.transcodeStatus && i.transcodeStatus !== 'ready' && i.transcodeStatus !== 'failed'). Add to the JSON response: transcodeNames (first TRANSCODE_LIST_CAP items' `title || name`, a bounded string[]) and transcodeOverflow (max(0, transcoding - transcodeNames.length)). Keep the existing `transcoding` count for backward compat. TRANSCODE_LIST_CAP = 10 (fork #6). public/js/setup.js pollScanStatus: extend the existing `if (s.transcoding > 0)` block (both in-progress and 'Scan complete' branches) to append `: ${s.transcodeNames.join(', ')}` plus, when transcodeOverflow>0, ` +${s.transcodeOverflow} more`; assign the whole message via statusText.textContent (NEVER innerHTML -- user-controlled filenames). Empty-set renders nothing new (existing >0 guard suppresses 'Converting 0'). No new HTML element, no new CSS.
tasks.done_when: GET /api/scan-status returns transcodeNames (cap 10) + transcodeOverflow, derived from the SAME needsTranscode/transcodeStatus filter as T2's codec-aware detection (integration test with a mixed flagged/unflagged fixture incl a codec-flagged HEVC .mp4); setup.js renders names via textContent; empty-set guard test (no 'Converting 0'); lint 0; Node 22 tests green.
tasks.gate: LIGHTER single-QA no-regression; payload-bounding gets focused attention

history.note: ENGINEERING-MANAGER finalized the task breakdown from PRINCIPAL-ENGINEER's Design (## Design + proposed ## Task breakdown in docs/exec-plans/active/2026-07-07-v1.18-ios-playability.md) and advanced to Implementation. Confirmed the PE's 4-task split (T1=FR-1a yt-dlp -S soft sort; T2=FR-1b codec probe + codec-aware needsTranscode + execFile hardening + lazy wiring; T3=FR-2 player poster/FOUC reset; T4=FR-3 bounded rescan transcode list). Applied the review tiers PM/PE recommended: T1 and T2 get the TWO-REVIEWER GATE (QA + adversarial /code-review) as the security-sensitive spawn touchpoints (yt-dlp arg-array core; ffprobe-on-user-files + larger transcode-spawn trigger surface) -- to PROVE no guard moved, none being loosened; T3 and T4 are LIGHTER single-QA no-regression, with Dean's on-device iPhone pass the ARBITER for FR-1/FR-2. Ran a file-overlap analysis for the SHARED working tree (no two agents may edit the same file concurrently): T1=lib/ytdlp/args.js, T2=server.js+helpers, T3=public/js/player.js are pairwise DISJOINT => START IN PARALLEL; T4=server.js+public/js/setup.js both DEPENDS ON T2 (must reuse T2's generalized codec-aware detection, not fork a divergent path) AND shares server.js with T2 => BLOCKED, start only after T2 is merged/verified. Finalized the exec plan's ## Task breakdown 'Ordering' to record start T1+T2+T3 concurrently, T4 after T2 (supersedes PE's suggested serial T1->T2->T4->T3). Set stage=implementation, current_tasks=[T1,T2,T3], T4 pending; populated the tasks[] array with per-task scope/files/done_when/gate/depends_on. Wrote self-contained task-suffixed inbox files .state/inbox/software-developer-T1.md ... -T4.md (each notes: coordinator owns ALL git -- SDEs report files-changed + Node 22 lint/test, do NOT commit; Node 22 toolchain bin path). Refreshed the stale .state/inbox/principal-engineer.md note (it still described a prior feature's T4 fix-round). Did not write application/test code; did not touch git. Route: run the three parallel software-developer sessions on T1/T2/T3, verify via build-specialist per task, then run T4 after T2.

PRODUCT-MANAGER completed Discovery for v1.18.0 'iOS playability + player polish,' a 3-FR focused branch (feature/v1.18-ios-playability, off main at v1.17.1) following v1.17.0 'Polish.'
